examples.text: Write a short message about your day (what you did and how you felt).

examples_answers.text: Today I went to work in the morning. I was very tired but the day was good. In the evening I rested at home.
Today day good. I work many. Tired. Home sleep.

examples.text: Describe your favourite hobbies and explain why you enjoy them.

examples_answers.text: My favourite hobbies are reading and swimming. I enjoy reading because it helps me relax after work. I also like swimming on weekends because it keeps me healthy and I can meet friends at the pool.
I have hobby is play game and music. I enjoy because is fun. I do every day one hour. Is my passion.

examples.text: Which sentence is grammatically correct?
options: She work in an office every day.
She works in an office every day.
She working in an office every day.
examples.answer: She works in an office every day.

examples.text: Choose the word that is closest in meaning to "difficult" in: "The exam was very difficult."
Describe an important day in your life and why it mattered to you.

examples_answers.text: The most important day in my life was when I graduated from university. I felt very proud because my family had supported me for years. We celebrated together, and I knew that moment would change my career opportunities.
The important day was when I get my diploma. I was very happy and my parents was proud too. We make party with friends. It was good memory for me.

examples.text: Which preposition completes the sentence correctly? "She is really interested ___ improving her pronunciation."
Explain step by step how you make a simple dish you often cook (ingredients and main stages).

examples_answers.text: First, I boil water in a pot and add a little salt. Then I put the pasta in the water and cook it for about ten minutes. After that, I drain the pasta and mix it with tomato sauce and cheese.
I make pasta easy. I put water in pot and make hot. After I put pasta and wait. Then I take out and put sauce. Is very good.

examples.text: Choose the correct past simple form: "Yesterday afternoon I ___ English vocabulary for an hour."
Describe the study environment where you learn English best (place, noise, tools, routine).

examples_answers.text: I learn English best in a quiet room with a clear desk and good light. I use my laptop for online exercises and keep my phone away so I can focus. Short breaks every forty minutes help me stay concentrated.
Best for me is place with no many noise and big window for light. I need computer for lesson. If is too loud I cannot study good.

examples.text: Which option correctly completes this second conditional? "If I ___ more free time, I would practise speaking every day."
Write about a trip or journey you remember well (where you went, what happened, how you felt).

examples_answers.text: Last year I took a train to visit a friend in another city. The journey was long but comfortable, and I enjoyed watching the countryside. When I arrived, we walked around the old town and tried local food. It was an exciting weekend I still remember clearly.
I go to trip in summer and was very nice. We see many thing and eat good food. The weather was sun and hot. I take many photos and was happy with my friends.

examples.text: Which collocation is natural in English?
They ___ in this city since 2020.
This email ___ sent to all students yesterday.
At the airport, the officer asked: "Could you ___ me your passport, please?"
Put these steps in a logical order for writing a short formal email in English.
elements: State clearly why you are writing.
Use an appropriate greeting (e.g. Dear Mr Smith,).
correct_order: Use an appropriate greeting (e.g. Dear Mr Smith,).
State clearly why you are writing.

examples.text: Order these parts of the day from earliest to latest.
What are the people in the image doing?
examples.image_description: A group of students studying together with books and laptops in a university library.

examples.text: Which option best describes what you see?
examples.image_description: A person working on a laptop at a small table in a café, with a cup of coffee nearby.
options: Someone working on a laptop in a café.
examples.answer: Someone working on a laptop in a café.

examples.text: Where is this scene most likely taking place?
examples.image_description: A teacher pointing at a whiteboard with grammar notes while learners sit at desks with notebooks.

examples.text: Introduce yourself in a few sentences: your name, where you are from, your job or studies, and one thing you like doing in English class or when you practise English.

examples_answers.text: Hello, my name is Ana and I am from Spain. I am a nurse and I study English online twice a week. In class I like speaking in pairs because it helps my fluency.
Hi I am Carlos. I from Mexico. I student. I like English class because fun.

examples.text: Give a balanced view: what are two advantages and two disadvantages of learning English mainly through apps and short videos? Use connectors such as however, on the other hand, and therefore where appropriate.

examples_answers.text: Learning English through apps and short videos has clear advantages: it is flexible, and you can study in short sessions every day. On the other hand, you often lack real interaction with a teacher who corrects your mistakes. Another disadvantage is that passive watching does not always improve speaking. Therefore, I combine apps with conversation practice whenever I can.
Apps is good because you can study when you want. Videos help listening. However the problem is you dont speak much. On other hand is cheap. So I think is ok but not enough alone.

metadata.question_types: writing_text, single_choice, fill_blank, ordering, speaking_record, image
metadata.rubric_note: CEFR-aligned dimensions mapped to criteria: task_realisation (task fulfillment), coherence (linkers/cohesion), sociolinguistic (register), lexicon (range/precision), morphosyntax (grammar range and accuracy).
